encounter.reason: Surgical site infection treatment, post laparoscopic appendectomy — readmission for IV antibiotics, wound management, and monitoring
encounter.location: Nebraska Medicine, 4350 Dewey Avenue, Omaha, NE 68105 — surgical floor

conditions.name: surgical site infection, right lower quadrant port site, post laparoscopic appendectomy
Escherichia coli wound infection with mixed anaerobes
perforated appendicitis, status post laparoscopic appendectomy

medications.name: piperacillin-tazobactam 4.5 g intravenous
medications.reason: surgical site infection, broad-spectrum empiric coverage

medications.name: ciprofloxacin 500 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: 500 mg by mouth twice daily for 10 days (through February 5, 2026)
medications.reason: surgical site infection — step-down from IV piperacillin-tazobactam, selected based on E. coli culture sensitivities

medications.name: metronidazole 500 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: 500 mg by mouth three times daily for 10 days (through February 5, 2026)

medications.name: acetaminophen 500 mg oral tablet
medications.sig: 500 mg by mouth every 6 hours as needed for pain

document_references.description: Comprehensive discharge summary authored by Dr. Priya Nair documenting the 4-day inpatient readmission for surgical site infection management following laparoscopic appendectomy. Covers hospital course including IV antibiotic therapy, wound management with bedside opening/irrigation/packing, trending labs and vitals showing clinical improvement, CT imaging ruling out drainable abscess, culture results guiding antibiotic step-down, and discharge plan with oral antibiotics and wound care instructions.

Reconciliation of inpatient medications to discharge prescriptions. Documents transition from IV piperacillin-tazobactam to oral ciprofloxacin plus metronidazole based on culture sensitivities, and transition from IV to oral acetaminophen for PRN pain.

Written wound care instructions for patient and his wife covering daily wound packing changes with saline-moistened gauze, wound hygiene, return-to-ED precautions (fever >101°F, worsening redness/drainage, increasing pain, systemic illness), activity restrictions (no heavy lifting, light duty permitted), and follow-up with Dr. Okonkwo on February 5, 2026.

CT abdomen/pelvis with IV contrast performed hospital day 2 to evaluate for intra-abdominal abscess. Findings: small 1.8 cm residual fluid collection in the right lower quadrant near prior appendiceal bed (decreased from initial 2.3 cm), no new or drainable abscess, no free fluid, post-surgical changes only. Interventional radiology not indicated.

diagnostic_reports.type: CT abdomen and pelvis with intravenous contrast
diagnostic_reports.description: Imaging performed to evaluate for intra-abdominal abscess in context of SSI. Demonstrated a small 1.8 cm residual fluid collection in the right lower quadrant near the prior appendiceal bed, decreased from initial 2.3 cm. No new or drainable abscess identified. No free fluid. Post-surgical changes only. Interventional radiology not consulted.

Culture from purulent drainage at right lower quadrant port site. Grew Escherichia coli (pan-sensitive) and mixed anaerobes. Sensitivities guided step-down from IV piperacillin-tazobactam to oral ciprofloxacin plus metronidazole. Specimen obtained at ED/admission encounter; resulted during this inpatient stay.
Blood cultures drawn at admission. Negative at 48 hours. Final no-growth report issued January 27, 2026. No bacteremia identified.
